source_evidence: Appendix B.1, Eq. (15)-(16): box defined by A_min, A_max; segment PQ with d = Q-P, X = P+td, 0<=t<=1. Sub-segment clipped by the 6 planes; t1 = (A_min.e1 - P.e1)/(d.e1), t2 = (A_max.e1 - P.e1)/(d.e1); if t1>t2 swap; t_min = max(t_min,t1), t_max = min(t_max,t2). 'The segment collides with the box if t_min <= t_max after all clipping.'
purpose: Tests whether a truss member (segment) intersects an axis-aligned box restriction primitive using a slab-clipping (Liang-Barsky style) algorithm.
assumptions: Box is axis-aligned, defined by extreme vertices A_min and A_max
Segment parameterized as X = P + td, 0 <= t <= 1
Initial t_min = 0, t_max = 1 positioning P' and Q' at P and Q
Procedure also handles the accidental case where A_min and A_max are reversed
formula_or_algorithm: d = Q - P. For each axis unit vector e_k (x,y,z): t1 = (A_min.e_k - P.e_k)/(d.e_k), t2 = (A_max.e_k - P.e_k)/(d.e_k); if t1 > t2 swap; t_min = max(t_min, t1); t_max = min(t_max, t2). After clipping on all three planes the sub-segment (hence the segment) collides with the box iff t_min <= t_max.
validation_opportunities: unit tests: segment inside, outside, grazing, and passing through the box
benchmark on crane example (box restriction zones)

reuse_risk.explanation: Well-known slab-clipping ray/box test with explicit equations; only care needed for division when d is parallel to a plane.

missing_information: Explicit handling when d.e_k = 0 (segment parallel to a slab)
